{{Quote|Looks like you have a ton of stuff weighing you down! Do you need to get rid of something?|Eocade, to a spacer that he was trying to buy items from|<ref name="SWG:TPS" />}}
'''Eocade''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] living during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He had [[Color/Legends|light brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and brown skin. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], he resided aboard [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]]. There, he was working as a [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]], buying [[Droid/Legends|droid]] parts, old weapons and various other things from [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] that passed by. He was one of the two junk dealers working on Tansarii Point Station, along with [[Anniya]], and he could frequently be found on the main level of the station, between the station's medical center and close combat training room where he bought item.<ref name="SWG:TPS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Eocade appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Eocade was added to the game with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 1|Chapter 1]]," on [[June 8]], [[2006]].<ref name="SonyForumsCite-Chapter 1: The Corellian Captives, Publish Notes, June 8th"> {{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/board/message?board.id=DevDiscussion&message.id=48548|text="Chapter 1: The Corellian Captives", Publish Notes, June 8th}}</ref> In the game, he was a junk dealer that the player could sell some, but not all, types of items to.<ref name="SWG:TPS" />
